positioning: Confidential Coordination Infrastructure for Organizations on Solana
reviewer_principle: Each specialist should review the lane that matches their protocol or provider. The ledger separates Ika/2PC-MPC, REFHE, ZK, commit-reveal, MagicBlock, QVAC, treasury, payout, RPC, Supabase, and hosted API evidence so one integration is not used to overclaim another.

integrations.name: Solana Testnet
integrations.role: Governance, proposal, vote, execution, proof network
integrations.boundary: Testnet-first; mainnet production custody is not claimed as complete.

integrations.name: Anchor
integrations.role: PrivateDAO program account model and instruction surface
integrations.boundary: Program-readiness evidence does not replace independent external audit.

integrations.name: ZK commit-reveal
integrations.role: Hide influence signals during active voting and reveal final outcome later
integrations.boundary: Current posture documents proof artifacts and reveal flow; on-chain verifier CPI is a stronger future path.

integrations.name: Public-private-until-reveal voting
integrations.role: Public proposal visibility with hidden counts, percentages, identities, intent, whale signals, and momentum during active voting
integrations.boundary: The UX posture is the active product rule; proof strength depends on the selected route and signed Testnet receipt.

integrations.name: Private rooms / VIP rooms
integrations.role: Invite-only coordination, private proposal notes, hidden member intent, reveal policy, proof export
integrations.boundary: Room names and private metadata must not be public; proof packets reveal only policy-approved outcome data.

integrations.name: Proof Center / Transparency Reports
integrations.role: Public verification after voting, payout, vesting, or execution completes
integrations.boundary: Transparency after completion does not expose hidden active-vote signals or private-room notes.

integrations.name: REFHE
integrations.role: Confidential payroll and encrypted execution proof lane
integrations.boundary: Proof/receipt oriented unless backed by fresh signed transaction or configured provider receipt.

integrations.name: Ika / 2PC-MPC / Encrypt lane
integrations.role: Future/optional approval and dWallet-style threshold-signing boundary for confidential operations
integrations.boundary: Ika/2PC-MPC is treated as a provider boundary and commitment lane unless final dWallet signing evidence is present.

integrations.name: MagicBlock
integrations.role: Private payment corridor and future ER/PER runtime lane
integrations.boundary: Current proof is PrivateDAO corridor; ER/PER-native delegation is labelled as upgrade path.

integrations.name: QVAC
integrations.role: Local/private decision intelligence before signing
integrations.boundary: Decision support only; hidden vote intent and private room transcripts are not sent by default.

integrations.name: GoldRush/Covalent
integrations.role: Treasury, wallet, and historical decision context
integrations.boundary: Optional provider; unavailable provider does not break the vote flow.

integrations.name: Arkham provider slot
integrations.role: Optional counterparty/entity risk context
integrations.boundary: Optional provider. No browser-exposed API key. Hidden vote data is excluded from provider requests.

integrations.name: Birdeye provider slot
integrations.role: Optional market and token context in the provider-router model
integrations.boundary: Provider slot is modular; unavailable state must not block governance.

integrations.name: Helius provider slot
integrations.role: Optional Solana account/transaction context and RPC resilience lane
integrations.boundary: Provider slot is future/optional unless configured.

integrations.name: Intelligence provider registry
integrations.role: Swap intelligence providers without changing the primary UX
integrations.boundary: Provider failures are isolated; default safe intelligence should work without external keys.

integrations.name: Umbra-compatible private payout
integrations.role: Private payout intent and receipt provider boundary
integrations.boundary: Sandbox fallback is labelled sandbox; no real Umbra settlement claim without configured receipt.

integrations.name: Cloak-style private settlement
integrations.role: Confidential settlement narrative and proof lane
integrations.boundary: Provider rail; not final real-funds production settlement without current receipt evidence.

integrations.name: Streamflow-compatible vesting
integrations.role: Confidential vesting rehearsal and future provider lane
integrations.boundary: Sandbox/provider boundary unless Streamflow config, signature, and receipt are present.

integrations.name: Private payout provider interface
integrations.role: Provider abstraction for payroll, vendor payout, grant payout, and reward payout
integrations.boundary: Provider receipts must not leak raw recipient metadata; sandbox receipts must remain labelled sandbox.

integrations.name: Jupiter
integrations.role: Treasury route preview and swap/liquidity corridor
integrations.boundary: Order preparation is separate from completed signed swap settlement.

integrations.name: PUSD / AUDD
integrations.role: Stablecoin treasury and payout context
integrations.boundary: Stablecoin support must be tied to configured mints, wallet signatures, and receipts for settlement claims.

integrations.name: Tokens-compatible asset context
integrations.role: Verify asset context before a vote or payout
integrations.boundary: Never blocks governance if provider is unavailable; fallback labels unknown/sandbox context.

integrations.name: Pyth-compatible price boundary
integrations.role: Price context for treasury, payout, and vesting decisions
integrations.boundary: Modular boundary; does not imply live oracle settlement unless configured.

integrations.name: Treasury coordination
integrations.role: Private treasury request, approval, route selection, execution receipt, public audit
integrations.boundary: Reveal public outcome and proof after completion, not active private coordination details.

integrations.name: Confidential payroll
integrations.role: Contributor payroll and reward proof lane with private metadata posture
integrations.boundary: Metadata/proof oriented unless live settlement receipt is attached.

integrations.name: GamingDAO rewards
integrations.role: Community and game reward coordination with private payout/reward rehearsal
integrations.boundary: Reward proof must separate game/community outcome from private recipient metadata.

integrations.name: QuickNode
integrations.role: RPC, stream telemetry, block/transaction ingestion and monitoring
integrations.boundary: Stream telemetry is monitoring evidence, not proposal-specific correctness proof.

integrations.name: Supabase
integrations.role: Operation receipts, analytics, room/proposal metadata, telemetry
integrations.boundary: Metrics must be reported only from returned data, not estimates.

integrations.name: AWS hosted API layer
integrations.role: Readiness, runtime, proof, and provider routes
integrations.boundary: Hosted API health is checked separately from static GitHub Pages mirrors.

integrations.name: Torque MCP
integrations.role: Growth-loop and event telemetry integration route
integrations.boundary: Engagement/growth infrastructure, not governance correctness proof.

integrations.name: AI-readable layer
integrations.role: Machine-readable reviewer and crawler surface
integrations.boundary: Manifests describe official surfaces and evidence; they do not replace source code or runtime verification.

reviewer_routing.question: How PrivateDAO frames Ika/2PC-MPC as a threshold-approval and confidential-operation boundary.
How encrypted payroll/payment proofs are represented and where the receipt boundary sits.
How the project separates commit, reveal, proof artifact, and future verifier CPI.
How active-vote influence signals are hidden until reveal.
How the current corridor works and what remains for ER/PER-native execution.
How intelligence runs before signing without hidden vote data.
How asset context, price context, routing, payout, and verification are connected.
Which proof is on-chain, runtime, repository, sandbox, or roadmap.

evaluation_rule: Prefer live on-chain proof and runtime APIs when present.
Use repository code and tests to verify provider boundaries and non-leakage behavior.
Treat sandbox lanes as product-ready rehearsals, not production settlement.
Treat roadmap lanes as explicit upgrade paths, not current completion claims.
Treat public UX as the simplified operator path and docs/reports as reviewer-grade detail.
